title: Getting Around Kaduna — Transport & Travel Guide
content:
To get around in Kaduna, you can use a combination of domestic flights, trains, buses, and city transport options such as taxis and ride-hailing services, depending on your budget and preferred mode of transportation.

[FACTBOX]
Main International Airport(s): Kaduna International Airport (KAD)
Domestic Air Network: moderate
Rail Network: limited
Drives On: right
Road Quality: poor
Ride-Hailing Apps: Bolt, local alternatives
[/FACTBOX]

[SECTION] Getting There [/SECTION]
Kaduna is accessible by air through the Kaduna International Airport, which receives flights from major Nigerian cities like Lagos and Abuja. You can also enter Kaduna by road from neighboring cities and states, including Abuja, Lagos, and Kano. The city is located along the Abuja-Kaduna-Zaria-Kano highway, making it a major stopover for road travelers. If you're coming from outside Nigeria, you'll likely need to fly into a major international airport like Lagos or Abuja and then take a domestic flight or bus to Kaduna.

The main overland entry points to Kaduna are the Abuja-Kaduna highway and the Kaduna-Kano highway. These highways are major routes that connect Kaduna to other parts of Nigeria, and you can find buses and taxis plying these routes. Alternatively, you can take a train from Abuja or Lagos to Kaduna, although the rail network is limited and the journey may take longer. It's worth noting that road travel in Nigeria can be challenging due to poor road conditions and traffic congestion, so it's essential to plan your journey carefully and allow for plenty of time.

[SECTION] Domestic Flights [/SECTION]
Domestic flights are a convenient way to get to Kaduna from other parts of Nigeria. Airlines like Aero Contractors, Arik Air, and Azman Air operate flights from major cities like Lagos and Abuja to Kaduna. You can book flights online or through a travel agent, and prices vary depending on the airline, time of year, and how far in advance you book. A one-way ticket from Lagos to Kaduna can cost between $50 to $200, depending on the airline and time of booking. It's a good idea to compare prices and book in advance to get the best deals.

[SECTION] Trains & Rail [/SECTION]
The rail network in Nigeria is limited, but you can take a train from Abuja or Lagos to Kaduna. The Nigerian Railway Corporation operates trains on these routes, and the journey can take several hours. The rail network is not extensive, and the trains may not be as frequent as buses or flights. However, taking the train can be a scenic and relaxing way to see the countryside, and you can enjoy the views of the Nigerian landscape. You can book train tickets online or at the train station, and prices vary depending on the class of service and route.

[SECTION] Buses & Coaches [/SECTION]
Buses and coaches are a popular way to get around in Nigeria, and you can find many bus companies operating in Kaduna. Companies like ABC Transport, God is Good Motors, and Young Shall Grow Motors operate buses from Kaduna to other parts of Nigeria. The buses are generally comfortable, and you can choose from different classes of service, including VIP and economy. Prices vary depending on the company, route, and class of service, but you can expect to pay between $10 to $50 for a one-way ticket from Kaduna to a nearby city.

[SECTION] City Transport [/SECTION]
| Mode | Available In | Cost (USD) | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| City Bus | Kaduna | $0.50-$1 | Inexpensive, but may not be comfortable |
| Taxi | Kaduna | $2-$5/km | Available everywhere, but prices may vary |
| Ride-Hailing | Kaduna | $5-$10 | Convenient, but may not be available everywhere |
| Keke Napep (tricycle) | Kaduna | $1-$2 | Inexpensive, but may not be comfortable |
| Okada (motorcycle) | Kaduna | $1-$2 | Inexpensive, but may not be safe |

[SECTION] Driving [/SECTION]
- To drive in Nigeria, you'll need a valid driver's license, either from your home country or a Nigerian license.
- Road conditions in Nigeria can be poor, with many potholes and uneven surfaces, so it's essential to drive carefully and slowly.
- Fuel costs in Nigeria can be high, so it's a good idea to budget accordingly and consider the cost of fuel when renting a car.
- Car rental companies are available in Kaduna, but it's essential to research and choose a reputable company to ensure you get a reliable vehicle.
- Driving in Nigeria can be challenging, especially for foreigners, so it's a good idea to hire a driver or take a taxi if you're not comfortable driving yourself.
- Be aware of your surroundings and keep an eye on your vehicle, as car theft and vandalism can be a problem in Nigeria.

[SECTION] Transport Tips [/SECTION]
- Always agree on the price before you start your journey, whether you're taking a taxi, bus, or ride-hailing service.
- Be careful of scams, especially when taking taxis or buses, and make sure you're getting into a legitimate vehicle.
- Download ride-hailing apps like Bolt to make it easier to get around Kaduna.
- Always wear a seatbelt and follow traffic rules to stay safe on the road.
- Be prepared for traffic congestion and delays, especially during peak hours.
- Consider hiring a driver or taking a taxi if you're not comfortable driving yourself, as driving in Nigeria can be challenging.
- Keep your valuables safe and secure, especially when taking public transportation, and be aware of your surroundings to avoid pickpocketing or theft.
- Learn some basic Nigerian phrases, such as "how much" and "where is," to help you navigate and communicate with locals.
